title: AVC errors with rootless podman after upgrade to Fedora 35
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**rootless podman AVC denied after upgrade to Fedora 35 (on only one system)**

<!--
I upgraded several systems to Fedora 35. I was successfully using rootless podman on all of them. After the upgrade, on only one of my systems, rootless podman cannot run anything because of AVC errors.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `$ podman run -it fedora /bin/bash`

**Describe the results you received:**

I just get my host system shell prompt back

**Describe the results you expected:**

A container shell prompt

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

```
----
time->Fri Nov  5 15:58:52 2021
type=AVC msg=audit(1636145932.303:896): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=9886 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c353,c937 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c353,c937 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
----
time->Fri Nov  5 15:58:52 2021
type=AVC msg=audit(1636145932.303:897): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=9886 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c353,c937 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c353,c937 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
----
time->Fri Nov  5 15:58:52 2021
type=AVC msg=audit(1636145932.303:898): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=9886 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c353,c937 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c353,c937 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
----
time->Fri Nov  5 15:58:52 2021
type=AVC msg=audit(1636145932.303:899): avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=9886 comm="bash" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c353,c937 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c353,c937 tclass=chr_file permissive=0
----
time->Fri Nov  5 15:58:52 2021
type=AVC msg=audit(1636145932.303:900): avc:  denied  { map } for  pid=9886 comm="bash" path="/usr/bin/bash" dev="fuse" ino=7102913 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c353,c937 tcontext=system_u:object_r:fusefs_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
```

I was successfully using rootless podman on this system with this user account before the upgrade to Fedora 35.
It works if I add `--security-opt label=disable`
It works on 3 other systems I upgraded to Fedora 35.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.4.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: update: I just realized it's happening on several of my systems. I was using `label=disable` for the containers on the other machines, so I didn't notice the failure
`touch /.autorelabel` and reboot seems to have fixed this problem on all my systems. I don't understand what changed from Fedora 34 to 35 to make that necessary.

I have been having similar issues also after upgrade to Fedora 35 on two different machines.  Here's what happens:
```
mydata$ time podman run --rm -itv "$(pwd)":/mydata:z --userns keep-id fedora date
Mon Nov  8 17:00:28 UTC 2021

real	1m45.685s
user	1m11.686s
sys	4m28.730s
mydata$ time podman run --rm -itv "$(pwd)":/mydata --userns keep-id fedora date
Mon Nov  8 17:00:44 UTC 2021

real	0m1.056s
user	0m0.108s
sys	0m0.078s
mydata$ time podman run --rm -itv "$(pwd)":/mydata:Z --userns keep-id fedora date
Mon Nov  8 17:02:51 UTC 2021

real	1m55.526s
user	1m13.472s
sys	5m18.963s
```
Note that without either `z` or `Z`, the command successfully executes in 1 second.  With either it takes almost two minutes!  After this test, I went up a directory and executed `restorecon -r -vv -F mydata`.  After that lengthy operation completed, I ran the tests above again and got the same result.

If $(PWD) is large it will take a while to relabel all of the files,  Not much we can do about this.

Those AVCs are allowed by default, you might have had an unsuccessful update of container-selinux. And eventually this cleared up.

Everything seems to be working the way it is supposed to now, so closing.
